background_jobs: Threading-based with database persistence. Job tracking via BackgroundJob model in src/models/league.py (~lines 1153-1180). Creates UUID-keyed rows in background_jobs table with status (running/completed/failed/cancelled), progress/total counters, current_player name, started_at, updated_at, results_json (JSON), error message. Multi-worker safe—all gunicorn workers query same DB state. Utilities in src/utils/background_jobs.py: create_background_job(job_type), update_job(job_id, **kwargs), get_job(job_id), is_job_cancelled(job_id). Auto-fails stale jobs after 4 hours. Example implementation in src/routes/api.py lines 4840-4883: admin_sync_all_player_fixtures spawns threading.Thread(target=_run_batch_sync), returns 202 with job_id for polling. Journey sync (JourneySyncService in src/services/journey_sync.py) and Academy sync (AcademySyncService in src/services/academy_sync_service.py) follow same pattern: fetch external API data, update progress, store results, handle cancellation. Frontend polls /api/admin/jobs/{job_id} for status/progress/current_player/results_json.
file_uploads: NONE CURRENTLY EXIST. No multipart/form-data endpoints, no Azure Blob Storage integration, no S3, no cloud storage, no MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH config in src/main.py. Only JSON POST bodies accepted (e.g., POST /newsletters/generate with data={} JSON). Feature requires building from scratch: (1) Add MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH config to Flask app, (2) Implement Azure Blob Storage client, (3) Create POST /api/admin/videos/upload multipart endpoint with @require_api_key, (4) Handle file streaming to blob storage in background thread to avoid blocking request, (5) Return blob_url for GPU pipeline reference. Recommend following the background job pattern: receive file → store in blob → create Job record → spawn thread that reads blob URL → submits to GPU service → polls results.
auth_model: Dual-factor admin auth via @require_api_key decorator (src/auth.py lines 119-229): (1) Bearer token with role="admin" issued by issue_user_token(email, role="admin"), max_age 30 days; (2) X-API-Key header matching ADMIN_API_KEY env var. Optional IP whitelist via ADMIN_IP_WHITELIST env var. Writer/journalist uses @require_user_auth decorator (lines 297-329)—just Bearer token, no API key. User model (UserAccount, lines 488-600 in league.py): email-based, has is_journalist, can_author_commentary, is_curator (editor), is_admin flags. NO team-level login—users are individual. JournalistTeamAssignment table (lines 851+) links journalists to teams they cover (many-to-many). NO "team account" concept where a team logs in and pays for service. Teams are Org data only. Implication: video feature should be admin-only (@require_api_key) or journalist-level (@require_user_auth, check is_journalist flag).
stripe_state: DEPRECATED / PARTIALLY REMOVED. Models still exist in schema but marked DEPRECATED: StripeConnectedAccount (table stripe_connected_accounts), StripeSubscriptionPlan (table stripe_subscription_plans), StripeSubscription (table stripe_subscriptions) all in src/models/league.py (~lines 900-950). Comment in code: "The Academy Watch refactor removed Stripe payment integration." No active Stripe webhook handlers, no Stripe API calls in src/services/, stripe_config.py exists but not imported in main.py. CONTINUITY.md lists Phase 1 refactor "Stripe removal" as done. If video processing requires billing, must either (1) resurrect/modernize Stripe integration, or (2) build new simple billing system (per-video credits, metered billing). Payment infrastructure dormant.
integration_points: Create new blueprint video.py in src/routes/ (follow pattern from src/routes/journey.py and academy.py). Add models VideoUpload and VideoAnalysisJob to src/models/league.py or new src/models/video.py. Create service VideoAnalysisService in src/services/video_analysis_service.py. Register blueprint in src/main.py line ~100 alongside other blueprints (journey_bp, academy_bp, etc.). Routes: POST /api/admin/videos/upload (multipart, @require_api_key) → creates VideoUpload + VideoAnalysisJob, spawns background thread; POST /api/admin/videos/{video_id}/analyze → submits to GPU; GET /api/admin/videos/{video_id} → fetch video + results; reuse existing GET /api/admin/jobs/{job_id} for polling. Frontend: new AdminVideos.jsx page (follow AdminNewsletters.jsx pattern), file upload input, job progress spinner, results display. Service layer: VideoAnalysisService.submit_to_gpu_pipeline(blob_url, callback), poll_gpu_job_status(gpu_job_id), fetch_results(gpu_job_id). All job tracking and auth infrastructure already in place.
